description:
Enable an aim to link to ANOTHER locally-checked-out repository AS A WHOLE — a black-box dependency: "that project supports this aim" without referencing its internals. Decision 2026-06-27: REPO-LEVEL links only. The earlier aim→aim model (target a specific external aim, render external aims, merged external-aim value flow, cross-repo aim search) is PARKED (halted children) — repo-level is more elegant and gives a clean modular border (you care about a project without knowing its internals).

DIRECTION & OWNERSHIP: a local aim is the supported (parent) side; the external repo is the supporter (child). You declare what supports you, never that another repo needs you. Weight stays on the local side; value flows OUT of this repo into the external repo. The other repo keeps NO back-reference.

IDENTITY: the edge stores just {repoId} (no aimId) — the "link the whole repo" form the old design had explicitly rejected, now embraced. Registry split unchanged: portable {repoId,url,name} in meta.json; machine-local {localPath,access} in .bowman/runtime/ (gitignored). Resolution: repoId → localPath; unresolved ⇒ "repo not checked out here" stub (name from portable registry), not a broken edge.

RENDERING: each linked repo is ONE node — a black box, tinted by the repo's meta color (neutral grey when not checked out), read-only, labeled by repo name. A flow-sized arrow runs from the repo node into the local aim. No internal aims are loaded or drawn — that opacity IS the modular border.

VALUE: the repo node is a leaf SINK. Merged calc = local aims (real intrinsic) + one zero-intrinsic node per linked repo + the repo edges, fed to the existing calculateAimValues. The repo node receives value only from inflow; local values shrink as flow is exported (intended — value is conserved flow, not aggregate). totalIntrinsic stays = local intrinsics.

Foundation (repoId + split portable/local registry) shipped in 1585aa46. v1 implementation = data-model + black-box rendering + repo-node value sink.
